notes:
Opening turn work notes: inspected current record with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence/read_evidence_range. Only juror-visible evidence is ev_54607018b65c_market-rules, sha256 54607018b65c81f4921890ad041c7d94def6c581d10571fdc1c8100c80c5a1e6, 1650 bytes. Case packet supplies market rules and Polymarket Yes rationale: Israel commenced offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Syria within 2024-09-12 to 2024-12-31 11:59 PM ET; Golan Heights count as Israeli territory; official confirmation by Syria/Israel/UN/P5 or consensus credible reporting; rationale says Netanyahu visited Syrian side of Mount Hermon on 2024-12-17 and said Israeli forces would remain until another security arrangement; reporting had earlier confirmed forces reached peak on 2024-12-08; Syrian peak not part of Golan Heights.

Issue outline: prove Israeli military action, within window, in Syria outside Israeli Golan Heights, intended to establish control over any portion of Syria, and acceptable authority/credible consensus. Plaintiff theory: Israeli entry and holding of Syrian side Mount Hermon/area of separation plus official remain/security-arrangement language satisfies preponderance. Defense likely argues limited temporary defensive deployment, not offensive; Israel UN letter says not intervening in Syrian conflict.

Native investigation search log for later admissible evidence: searched web for Netanyahu 2024-12-17 Syrian side of Mount Hermon statement; Israeli forces reached Syrian Mount Hermon 2024-12-08; Israel/Syria buffer zone official UN confirmation; site:press.un.org and site:un.org UNDOF/Israel/Syria December 2024; site:gov.il Netanyahu Hermon. Key source targets/leads: UNDOF press statement 2024-12-13 at peacekeeping.un.org/en/news/undof-press-statement (UNDOF observed significant IDF movements since Dec 7, confirmed IDF remained in area of separation in multiple locations as of Dec 13, told Israel violation of 1974 Agreement); Israel UN letter S/2024/887 dated Dec 9 at documents.un.org/doc/undoc/gen/n24/386/65/pdf/n2438665.pdf and digitallibrary.un.org/record/4069324 (Israel says limited/temporary measures, IDF deployed east of Line A in few points, defensive rationale, not intervention); DPPA/UN News Dec 12 item calling for withdrawal from Golan buffer zone; UN AV 9826th meeting Dec 20 re reports of Israel incursion; Times of Israel/Ynet/Reuters-linked reporting for Netanyahu statement; JPost/TOI reporting on Dec 8 capture. Later phase should submit primary official material before relying on it.

Adverse check: Israel's own letter may narrow intent/offensive element. Need answer by showing market definition does not require hostile motive beyond military offensive intended to control territory, and holding Syrian territory until security conditions change is control. Missing links to admit later: official PM/GPO statement for Dec 17, UNDOF statement, Israel UN letter, geography support for Syrian peak outside Golan if challenged.

Defense opening turn notes, arb-ex04-20260629225838. Opportunity openings:defendant ready; final action record_opening_statement only; no evidence submission allowed. Inspected get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Current visible record has one evidence item only: ev_54607018b65c_market-rules, market-rules.md, case_packet, text/markdown, 1650 bytes, sha256 54607018b65c81f4921890ad041c7d94def6c581d10571fdc1c8100c80c5a1e6. Full read confirms it contains Polymarket question/guidelines and Polymarket Yes rationale: Netanyahu visited Syrian side of Mount Hermon on Dec. 17, 2024; said Israeli forces would remain until another security arrangement; credible reporting had confirmed Israeli forces reached peak on Dec. 8; Syrian peak of Mount Hermon is not part of Golan Heights. Assessment: evidence cleanly proves market terms and the administrator's rationale, but not the underlying source chain; it does not attach official Israeli/UN statements, maps, or reporting. Plaintiff opening relies on rationale and expects later proof. Investigation before opening: web searches for Dec 2024 Israel Syria buffer zone/Mount Hermon/Netanyahu/UN/UNDOF. Leads: official PMO pages https://www.gov.il/en/pages/event-hermon171224 and https://www.gov.il/en/pages/event-golan081224 appear directly material, but simple urllib fetch returned HTTP 403; target later with browser/capture if evidence submission allowed. AP Dec. 9 2024 page opened: reports seizure of demilitarized buffer zone in Syria, temporary/security framing, UN spokesman saying UNDOF warned the actions would violate the 1974 agreement, and Israeli leaders saying not a prelude to entering other parts of Syria. UN/press search found later Security Council material but not yet an admitted Dec 2024 UNDOF source. Defense theory for opening: concede current record creates a serious source target for plaintiff but emphasize burden and missing admitted proof on three links: exact geography outside the Golan carveout, actual military entry/control by deadline, and whether the conduct qualifies as an offensive intended to establish control rather than temporary defensive deployment. Need later submit evidence: PMO Dec 8/17 pages, UN/UNDOF Dec 2024 or first report after Dec 8, AP/credible reporting, geographic source, and adverse temporary/defensive/no-border-change sources. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Arguments turn notes: opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Record scan with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence: only original case packet existed before plaintiff submissions; defendant opening argued geography, offensive, intent, temporary/defensive framing.

Investigation/capture: UNDOF page successfully fetched by Python urllib from https://peacekeeping.un.org/en/news/undof-press-statement (200, text/html, ~213KB). Extracted with small HTMLParser; key content: since 07 Dec IDF movements within area of separation and along ceasefire line; as of 13 Dec IDF remain in area of separation in multiple locations; UNDOF informed Israel these actions violate 1974 Disengagement Agreement. Israel letter PDF S/2024/887 downloaded from documents.un.org (200, application/pdf, 144396 bytes), but no pdftotext and no Python PDF libraries; attempted python3 -m pip install --user pypdf, failed no pip module; did not rely on PDF content. Gov.il direct local fetch returned HTTP 403 for event-us121224, event-hermon171224, event-golan081224; web search/open exposed indexed text, submitted concise captures with limitation notes. Times of Israel liveblog direct local fetch failed HTTP 403; not submitted. Jerusalem Post article fetched locally and text extracted; Ynet article fetched locally and text extracted.

Evidence admitted in this opportunity: ev_ef202b15c89e_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-ef202b15c89e UNDOF press statement; ev_15b73465e3b7_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-15b73465e3b7 Israel PMO 12 Dec buffer-zone control statement; ev_0d98a74d5144_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-0d98a74d5144 Israel PMO 17 Dec Mount Hermon deployment statement; ev_63f356380079_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-63f356380079 Jerusalem Post 8 Dec Syrian side Mount Hermon takeover; ev_c2dd3776f3e3_submitted-evidence-05-plaintiff-c2dd3776f3e3 Ynet 17 Dec Syrian side Mount Hermon; ev_be16a363f499_submitted-evidence-06-plaintiff-be16a363f499 Israel PMO 8 Dec Golan statement/order to take over positions with temporary/defensive framing.

Evidence analysis: Case packet proves rules and Polymarket rationale, but derivative. UNDOF provides strong official UN confirmation of IDF presence/movement inside area of separation and violation. Israel PMO provides official Israel confirmation of orders to take over positions/buffer zone in Syria and continued deployment/stay until arrangement, while also preserving adverse temporary/defensive framing. JPost/Ynet provide credible reporting for Syrian side Mount Hermon and dates. Missing/adverse: gov.il captures are from indexed/rendered text due to 403 local fetch; Israel letter PDF not text extracted; precise map/geography could be further strengthened if rebuttal needed. Legal theory: defensive purpose/temporary duration does not defeat market trigger because market asks control over any portion of Syria; official words 'take control', 'take over', 'continued deployment', and 'remain' establish intended control by preponderance.

Defense merits argument notes. Opportunity arguments:defendant; final action submit_argument; evidence submission permitted. Inspected get_case/list_evidence and stat/read key items. Plaintiff added PX-1 market rules plus six sources. Full reads: UNDOF 13 Dec (ev_ef202...) confirms significant IDF movements in area of separation and along ceasefire line; as of 13 Dec IDF remained in area of separation in multiple locations; UNDOF said this violates 1974 agreement and urged cease military activities. It proves presence/violation but not the market term offensive or exact Mount Hermon geography. PMO 12 Dec (ev_15b...) says Netanyahu ordered IDF to temporarily take control of buffer zone in Syria until effective force enforces 1974 Separation of Forces Agreement; strong on control, strong on temporary/security framing. PMO 17 Dec (ev_0d98...) says Netanyahu at summit of Mt Hermon, operational briefing, directives on continued deployment, decide on deployment until arrangement ensures Israel security; adverse on continued deployment but still security framing. PMO 8 Dec (ev_be16...) says order to take over positions, temporary defensive position until arrangement; beyond border in Syria. JPost (ev_63f...) reports IDF took over Syrian side of Mount Hermon range to expand buffer zone; military would not get involved in internal occurrences in Syria unless threat to Israeli security. Ynet (ev_c2...) reports Netanyahu visited Syrian side of Mount Hermon and Israel/troops will remain until arrangement. Submitted defense evidence AP Dec 9 2024 analysis; accepted as ev_04b4d4fac724_submitted-evidence-07-defendant-04b4d4fac724. AP preserves both sides: confirms seizure/control/UN violation but quotes temporary defensive/targeted framing, military official saying temporary step to keep stability and UN forces, section 'Is Israel invading Syria?' saying leaders stressed seizure was temporary and not prelude to entering other parts of Syria, analyst calling it tactical not long-term strategy and not trying to change border or prepare invasion. Strategy: do not deny record proves some control/presence in Syria; argue plaintiff conflates temporary defensive control with 'military offensive intended to establish control.' Preserve provenance limitation for gov.il captures: concise indexed/rendered captures after HTTP 403, not direct-fetched full pages. Offer AP plus selected plaintiff exhibits to distinguish record. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Rebuttal turn notes: record scanned with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. Defendant submitted AP evidence ev_04b4d4fac724_submitted-evidence-07-defendant-04b4d4fac724. Full AP evidence bytes read with read_evidence_range. Opponent’s strongest points: temporary/defensive/tactical framing; argument that 'offensive' and 'intent to establish control' require more than buffer-zone presence; gov.il captures are indexed/rendered captures due to HTTP 403 local fetch. Rebuttal analysis: AP evidence itself confirms control: Israeli military began seizing control; Netanyahu said forces moving to control about 400 sq km demilitarized buffer zone in Syrian territory; Foreign Minister Saar said IDF took targeted and temporary control of certain areas. 'Temporary' and 'defensive' limit duration/motive but do not negate intended control. Market says any portion of Syria and does not require annexation, border change, permanent occupation, or entry into other parts of Syria. UNDOF's violation framing and AP's seizing-control language support an offensive/advance into an area where military forces should not be. No new source needed; offer defendant AP exhibit as PX-R1 if useful.

Defense surrebuttal notes. Opportunity surrebuttals:defendant; final action submit_surrebuttal/pass. Re-checked get_case/list_evidence. No new evidence since defendant AP exhibit and plaintiff rebuttal; evidence list unchanged. Plaintiff rebuttal argues defense creates false choice: temporary/defensive can still satisfy control; uses defense AP exhibit as PX-R1 for 'seizing control,' 'moving to control,' and 'targeted and temporary control'; says temporary control is still intent to control and defensive purpose does not negate offensive; says gov.il captures should be weighed for both limiting words and control words. Surrebuttal plan: concede temporary control can be control; stress the separate market requirements 'military offensive' and 'intended to establish control' cannot be collapsed into any cross-border control. AP's 'Is Israel invading Syria?' section and temporary/tactical/no-border-change/no-prelude-to-entering-other-parts evidence goes to offensive characterization, not just permanence. UNDOF proves presence and treaty violation, not offensive intent. Gov.il exhibits are mixed and must be read as whole statements with limiting temporary/defensive/security wording. No new evidence needed; existing record enough. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Closing turn notes: record scanned with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. No new evidence after defendant AP evidence and defendant surrebuttal. Closing cannot add evidence or technical reports. Decisive evidence: market rule/rationale; Israeli official statements saying take over positions, take control of buffer zone in Syria, continued deployment on Mount Hermon until arrangement; UNDOF official statement that IDF moved/remained in area of separation and actions violated 1974 agreement; credible reports identifying Syrian side Mount Hermon; defendant AP exhibit confirming seizing/moving/targeted temporary control. Strongest contrary point is temporary/defensive/security/tactical framing. Response: the market does not require permanent, annexationist, border-changing, or non-defensive control; it requires a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Syria. Military advance into Syrian territory and the area of separation, with taking/holding control until a condition is met, satisfies preponderance. Source-weight note: gov.il captures were limited by 403, but defense also relies on them; UNDOF/JPost/Ynet/AP are cleaner corroboration.

Defense closing notes. Opportunity closings:defendant; final action deliver_closing_statement only; no evidence admission. Re-checked get_case and list_evidence: eight evidence items, no new evidence after defendant AP exhibit; plaintiff closing filed. Plaintiff closing concedes no need for annexation/permanence/broader invasion; relies on take over/take control/continued deployment/remain; says temporary/defensive only explain duration and motive; uses UNDOF for movements/remained/violation; uses JPost/Ynet/market packet for geography; AP for seizure/control while acknowledging temporary/no broader invasion. Closing plan: candidly concede record likely proves Syrian-side temporary control and UNDOF violation; re-center exact trigger 'commences a military offensive intended to establish control,' not any temporary security control. Most authoritative intent evidence is Israeli official wording: temporary, defensive, security, until effective force enforces 1974 agreement, until suitable arrangement, ensures Israel security. That points to preserving/replacing status quo amid security vacuum, not establishing control as market-defined invasion. UNDOF proves violation/presence, not offensive/invasion. AP context matters because it asked whether invading and recorded not a prelude to entering other territory, not border change, tactical temporary operation. Gov.il snippets are partial but should be read as whole snippets. Final ask: No because plaintiff's interpretation erases 'military offensive' and makes any temporary buffer-zone violation count. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
